consistency.notes: All non-null/non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text. The product title, producer, origin, process, varieties, altitude, harvest, roast profile, tasting notes, pricing, currency, package grams, and bag count are explicitly stated. The roaster location 'Portland, Oregon' is supported by the footer address, and roaster country 'United States' follows from 'Portland, Oregon, USA'. Boolean labels are supported by the page: single-origin/farm coffee implies not a blend; no espresso, decaf, or coferment/infused indication appears; 'washed' process is explicit. 'is_specialty_coffee' is supported by the single-origin detailed coffee product presentation with origin/producer/process/variety/altitude/harvest/tasting-note specificity.

realism.reasons: Very plausible as an html-to-text render of a real wholesale roaster product page. The structure matches ecommerce extraction well: nav/header remnants, wholesale notice, title, default selection, option groups, origin/spec fields, tier pricing, descriptive copy, and a footer. The coffee details are coherent and realistic for a Guatemala Huehuetenango washed lot: producer/finca, Bourbon/Caturra, 1,500–1,800 masl, seasonal harvest timing, and flavor notes all fit specialty-coffee conventions. The wholesale pricing logic is internally consistent, with the top price matching the default variant and discounted case pricing explained clearly. Variant block also feels authentic: ids, skus, titles, duplicated option combinations, and package weights are all sensible. The gram conversions are precise in a machine-generated way that is especially believable for embedded product data. Voice is solid—professional, slightly marketing-oriented, but not overblown. Only tiny tells keep it from being absolutely indistinguishable: the brand/contact info uses an example-domain email, and ‘Custom packing available by request’ plus some copy feels a bit neatly composed for a stripped page export. But overall this reads like a real Shopify-style wholesale coffee product page.

label.coffee.coffee_name: Guatemala La Bolsa — Washed Bourbon & Caturra
label.coffee.sensory_text:
Tasting notes: red apple, caramel, orange blossom

This washed lot from Finca La Bolsa is a clean, structured Guatemala we roast for everyday wholesale menus. In the cup we find red apple and orange blossom up front, with a caramel sweetness that carries through a crisp finish. It is approachable enough for batch brew but still has the clarity we want on a rotating single-origin bar.
label.coffee.producer_text: La Bolsa sits high in Huehuetenango, where cool nights and limestone-rich soils help the Bourbon and Caturra trees develop dense, sweet cherries. The coffee is depulped, fermented, washed, and dried slowly on patios before being prepared for export.

coffee.producer_text: This washed lot from Finca La Bolsa is a clean, structured Guatemala we roast for everyday wholesale menus. La Bolsa sits high in Huehuetenango, where cool nights and limestone-rich soils help the Bourbon and Caturra trees develop dense, sweet cherries.
